bugs.description: Ownership check in read_item inverted (!= becomes ==): a non-superuser can now read any other user's item by id, and is blocked from reading their own. Classic IDOR / broken object-level authorization.

The owner-scoping filter (.where(Item.owner_id == current_user.id)) was removed from the non-superuser list query, so a normal user's GET /items now returns every user's items. Multi-tenant data leak.
The 'exp' (expiration) claim was dropped from the JWT payload, so access tokens never expire. A leaked token is valid forever.

The current-password verification was removed from update_password_me, so an authenticated session can set a new password without proving knowledge of the current one (enables full account takeover after a session hijack/CSRF).

Self-delete guard inverted (if current_user.is_superuser -> if not current_user.is_superuser). Normal users can no longer delete their own account, while superusers can now delete themselves -- the opposite of the intended rule.
Email-uniqueness check inverted (!= becomes ==): updating to your OWN email is now rejected as a conflict, while taking ANOTHER existing user's email is allowed, creating duplicate emails.

reset_password now returns 404 'User not found' instead of the deliberately-generic 'Invalid token' when the email has no account, re-introducing a user-enumeration oracle on the password reset endpoint.

injection.find:
# Don't reveal that the user doesn't exist - use same error as invalid token
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid token")
injection.replace_with:
# Don't reveal that the user doesn't exist - use same error as invalid token
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="User not found")

bugs.description: PATCH update_item switched from exclude_unset=True to exclude_unset=False, so fields the client did NOT send are included in the update dict as their defaults and overwrite existing values -- a partial update now silently wipes untouched fields.

update_user_me binds the body to UserUpdate instead of UserUpdateMe. UserUpdate (via UserBase) includes is_superuser/is_active, and current_user.sqlmodel_update applies every supplied field -- so any user can PATCH /users/me with {"is_superuser": true} and self-escalate to admin (mass-assignment / over-posting).

injection.find: *, session: SessionDep, user_in: UserUpdateMe, current_user: CurrentUser
injection.replace_with: *, session: SessionDep, user_in: UserUpdate, current_user: CurrentUser

bugs.description: delete_user's cascade filter is inverted: delete(Item).where(col(Item.owner_id) == user_id) became != user_id. Deleting one user now wipes EVERY OTHER user's items and orphans the deleted user's -- catastrophic cross-tenant data loss.
